hero.title: The infinite AI canvas,
hero.subtitle: Break free from locked-in ecosystems like TapNow, LibTV, and Higgsfield. Bring your own key and generate, manipulate, and direct images and videos on a spatial workspace.

providers.eyebrow: Connect any API. Zero platform lock-in.

how.title: How Open Canvas works
how.subtitle: A visual workspace built for multi-modal AI. No locked credit system, no hidden workflow format, no single-provider ceiling.

steps.title: 1. Bring your own key
steps.body: Connect OpenAI, Gemini, OpenRouter, Replicate, or Cyberbara. You keep provider access and billing in your own account.

steps.title: 2. Add nodes
steps.body: Place text, image, video, and generation nodes on a spatial canvas so your creative workflow stays visible.

steps.title: 3. Connect and generate
steps.body: Turn one output into the next input. Compose multi-model image and video pipelines without locking work into one platform.

quick.title: Quick start
quick.subtitle: Use the hosted app when you want speed. Self-host when you want complete control.
quick.hostedBody: The fastest way to try Open Canvas. Use the Cyberbara-hosted app, keep your workflow in the browser, and bring your own API keys.
quick.selfHostedBody: Ask your desktop AI agent to install and run the project locally. It can read the repository, install dependencies, and start the app for you.
quick.promptLabel: # Send this command to your AI agent:
quick.promptText: Setup and run Open Canvas on the desktop for me. Read

items.question: What is BYOK?
items.answer: BYOK means Bring Your Own Key. Instead of buying platform credits, you paste your provider API keys and pay the provider directly for the generations you run.

items.question: Is Open Canvas free?
items.answer: Yes. The product is open source and the hosted app is free to use. Your only generation cost is whatever your selected AI provider charges for API usage.

items.question: Can I self-host it?
items.answer: Yes. You can run Open Canvas locally or deploy your own copy. Canvas JSON import and export keeps your work portable.

items.question: Are my API keys safe in the hosted app?
items.answer: Your keys are configured in your browser and used for provider requests. For the strictest control, run the same open-source app locally or on your own infrastructure.
